query: I've finished a Rust change on this branch. Do a Rust-only code review of the diff against main — run the cargo gate, apply the severity checklist, and tell me if it's mergeable with an Approve/Warning/Block verdict.
expected_behavior: Selects rust-review (or the review workflow pinned to Rust).
Establishes the cargo quality gate (fmt/clippy/test) before judging.
Ends with an explicit Approve/Warning/Block verdict, not just a list of comments.

query: Before I publish this crate, review its public API against the Rust API Guidelines — naming, error types, and whether the surface is future-proof.
expected_behavior: Selects rust-review and runs its public-API design pass (api-design.md against the C-guidelines).
Does not treat this as a generic idioms polish; it is a review-verdict task.
May cite rust-idioms / rust-ecosystem as owners for specific fixes, but rust-review drives.

query: Review my flake.nix and default.nix changes before I merge them.
expected_behavior: Selects nix-review, NOT rust-review — the diff is Nix even though the ask is 'review before merge'.
Runs the Nix gate (nix flake check, statix, deadnix, formatter --check).
Keyword overlap on 'review'/'merge' must not pull the Rust reviewer.

query: My Rust test passes locally but fails intermittently in CI — help me track down this heisenbug.
expected_behavior: Selects debugging for the Rust-specific toolbox (repro shrinking, loom/Miri for heisenbugs, RUST_BACKTRACE).
Focuses on reproducing and localizing the flake before proposing a fix.
Does not jump to rust-concurrency internals until a root cause is found (may cite it once localized).

query: A regression showed up somewhere in the last 40 commits. I want to bisect to find exactly which commit broke it.
expected_behavior: Selects debugging and reaches for git bisect / cargo bisect-rustc.
Treats bisection as localization, not a code-review or refactoring task.

query: I'm getting compile error E0499: cannot borrow `buf` as mutable more than once at a time. How do I restructure this?
expected_behavior: Selects rust-ownership, NOT debugging — a borrow-checker error is an ownership fix, not a runtime bug hunt.
Explains the borrow conflict and offers a concrete restructuring.
The words 'error' and 'failing to compile' must not route this to the debugging toolbox.

query: The rust-audit report came back with about a dozen findings. Help me triage them and work through the fixes one by one.
expected_behavior: Selects addressing-findings (its gather → normalize → triage → fix → verify loop), optionally scaling to triage-findings.
Delegates the how-to-fix of each finding to the relevant topic skill.
Does not itself re-run the review rubric; it consumes existing findings.

query: There are a bunch of unresolved reviewer comments on my PR. Walk me through resolving them and closing the threads on GitHub.
expected_behavior: Selects addressing-findings — the fix loop for review findings and PR comments.
Normalizes comments to one schema and triages (accept/reject/defer/needs-decision/conflict).
Closes the GitHub loop at the end.

query: Review my diff and tell me what's wrong with it before I commit.
expected_behavior: Selects rust-review (produce findings + verdict), NOT addressing-findings — no findings exist yet to fix.
addressing-findings is the downstream fix loop; do not invoke it to generate a review.

query: Add property-based tests with proptest for this parser, plus a couple of table-driven rstest cases.
expected_behavior: Selects rust-testing (proptest, rstest, test layout).
Writes real tests rather than only describing them.
Does not drift into perf benchmarking.

query: How do I write an async integration test with #[tokio::test] and wire nextest into CI?
expected_behavior: Selects rust-testing (async tests, integration tests, nextest, CI wiring).
Covers the runner setup, not just a single test body.

query: I need a criterion benchmark to measure how fast this hashing function is.
expected_behavior: Selects rust-performance, NOT rust-testing — benchmarks live in the performance skill.
Sets up criterion (or divan) with a measure-first framing.
The word 'measure' must not route this to the testing skill.

query: Should I use thiserror or anyhow for this library's error type, and how should I structure the variants?
expected_behavior: Selects rust-errors (thiserror-vs-anyhow decision, library-vs-app error design).
Recommends thiserror for a library and explains variant design/opacity.

query: Design a retry-with-backoff and a circuit breaker around this flaky network call so it degrades gracefully.
expected_behavior: Selects rust-errors (recovery: retry, fallback, circuit breaker).
Frames the failure as a domain failure to recover from, not a defect to panic on.

query: A worker thread panicked while holding the Mutex and now every other thread hits a PoisonError. How do I handle the poisoning?
expected_behavior: Selects rust-concurrency, NOT rust-errors — lock poisoning mechanics are owned by the concurrency skill.
Explains PoisonError recovery / into_inner and that a poisoned lock signals a prior defect.
The words 'error' and 'handle' must not route this to rust-errors.

query: The borrow checker rejects this with E0502 (cannot borrow as immutable because it is also borrowed as mutable). Help me restructure it.
expected_behavior: Selects rust-ownership and explains the E0502 conflict.
Offers a concrete restructuring (split borrows, scope the mutable borrow, etc.).

query: In this single-threaded tree, should I reach for Rc<RefCell<T>>, Box<T>, or Cow<'a, str>? I keep guessing.
expected_behavior: Selects rust-ownership (smart pointers, interior mutability, Cow).
Frames the choice around single-threaded ownership, not cross-thread sharing.
Does not select rust-concurrency — there is no threading here.

query: I want to share a counter across several tokio tasks and mutate it safely. Arc<Mutex>? And I'm getting 'future is not Send'.
expected_behavior: Selects rust-concurrency, NOT rust-ownership — cross-task sharing and Send/Sync are concurrency.
Explains Arc<Mutex> sharing and diagnoses the 'future is not Send' error (e.g. a non-Send guard held across .await).
The presence of Arc/Mutex must not misroute this to the single-threaded ownership skill.

query: Two of my threads deadlock intermittently. I think I'm holding a lock across an .await point. Help me fix it.
expected_behavior: Selects rust-concurrency (deadlocks, holding a lock across await).
Explains why holding a std/parking_lot guard across .await deadlocks or breaks Send, and how to scope it.

query: How do I fix E0382: use of moved value `config`?
expected_behavior: Selects rust-ownership, NOT rust-concurrency — a move error is single-value ownership.
Explains the move and offers clone/borrow/restructure options.

query: Is this Nix change mergeable? Run nix flake check and give me a verdict on the flake.
expected_behavior: Selects nix-review (flake quality gate + verdict).
Runs nix flake check / formatter --check / statix / deadnix and issues Approve/Warning/Block.
Checks purity/reproducibility, not just formatting.

query: Review this flake for impure derivations and import-from-derivation before I merge it.
expected_behavior: Selects nix-review and specifically checks purity/reproducibility (impure derivations, IFD).
Applies the Nix severity checklist and returns a verdict.

query: Review my axum handler and sqlx query changes before I merge this PR.
expected_behavior: Selects rust-review, NOT nix-review — axum/sqlx is Rust.
Runs the Rust cargo gate and severity checklist.
May cite rust-web for domain specifics, but the review engine is Rust.

query: This hot loop is slow. Profile it, find where the time goes, and cut the heap allocations.
expected_behavior: Selects rust-performance (measure-first, profiling, allocation reduction).
Insists on measuring/profiling before optimizing.
Does not treat this as a correctness bug hunt.

query: My Rust build takes four minutes on every edit. How do I cut compile times — mold, sccache, codegen-units, incremental?
expected_behavior: Selects rust-performance (compile-time / edit-compile-run loop tuning: mold/lld, sccache, codegen-units).
Addresses build speed, distinct from runtime performance.

query: My program gives the wrong answer only when the input is large. Help me find the bug.
expected_behavior: Selects debugging, NOT rust-performance — 'large input' is about correctness, not speed.
Reproduces and localizes the wrong-answer bug (shrink the repro) before proposing a fix.
The words 'large input' / 'only when' must not route this to the performance skill.

query: Turn this vague feature request into concrete Given/When/Then acceptance scenarios before we write any code.
expected_behavior: Selects specs (specification by example, Given/When/Then, outside-in).
Produces concrete example scenarios that double as acceptance criteria.
Language-agnostic — does not jump straight to a Rust test framework.

query: Help me clarify the acceptance criteria for the checkout flow using BDD, working outside-in.
expected_behavior: Selects specs (BDD/ATDD acceptance criteria, outside-in).
Focuses on defining behavior/examples, not on wiring a runner.

query: I already have these Gherkin scenarios written. Set up the cucumber runner so they actually execute in my Rust project.
expected_behavior: Selects rust-testing, NOT specs — running Gherkin scenarios (cucumber) is owned by the testing skill.
Wires up the cucumber crate and step definitions.
'Gherkin' must not route this to specs, which only owns writing the scenarios.
